summary: The prior AC8 gap is resolved and all five verification kinds plus the validation sequence are strict-current-HEAD and passing, but the E2E evidence still counts a hard-coded acceptance-prose test as one of its five public CLI scenarios and overclaims S-001 through S-005 / AC-1 through AC-8 coverage.
inspection_summary: Reviewed the current gate_evidence request, the e7921b85..e7ce2b31 repair diff, Story/Architecture/Spec/test plan, production owner and guarded-run tests, the full workflow-heavy E2E source, all five current verification records and artifacts, validation sequencing state, responsibility/failure-mode gates, architecture conformance, current review records, and adjudication artifacts. Re-ran the current E2E command successfully. The AC8 repair is substantive and non-circular: scenario:S-005 now reads the Autonomous Action DAG, Production Runtime Connectors, Independent Review Orchestration, final Story, and roadmap files; asserts predecessor completion with PR #372/#377/#382; requires exact pre-PR active status for the final Story and roadmap; and verifies the autonomous plan omits pr_create, merge, and waiver. Current unit 288/288, integration 4/4, E2E 5/5, typecheck, and build records are verified and strict-bound to e7ce2b312147c3f10bd2c1b56b1586151cc2c51d. All five validation phases pass on the same frozen HEAD/fingerprint; responsibility_authority and failure_mode_coverage have sufficient current evidence; conformance improves origin/main 81 to 80 with no new run-session-to-CLI reverse dependency. The remaining evidence defect is that the fifth E2E test still validates hard-coded Japanese acceptance prose rather than runtime or repository behavior. The E2E ledger nevertheless describes all five tests as public CLI scenarios and claims post-freeze S-001 through S-005 and AC-1 through AC-8 replay, although the named behavioral tests are S-001, S-002, S-004, and S-005 and the hard-coded test is the only in-file marker for the remaining labels. Existing adjudication records are stale and current-head adjudication is correctly still pending; they were not accepted as proof.
judgment_delta: Prior AC8 non-circular staged-closure finding -> resolved because scenario:S-005 now inspects all three predecessor Story SSOTs, asserts PR #372/#377/#382, exact pre-PR active states, and forbidden-action absence.

Exact-head verification freshness concern -> resolved because unit, integration, E2E, typecheck, and build records and their verified artifacts are strict-bound to e7ce2b312147c3f10bd2c1b56b1586151cc2c51d.

Validation/Gate/conformance concern -> resolved because all five validation phases pass on the frozen current HEAD, responsibility and all six failure modes are covered, and conformance improves 81 to 80 without a new CLI reverse dependency.

Claim that the current E2E has no circular Story-prose marker -> not resolved because the final acceptance-coverage test still matches hard-coded prose and is counted in the 5/5 artifact and AC/S scenario claim.

findings.detail: test/e2e/story-vibepro-one-command-pr-ready-closure-runtime.spec.ts still contains `story-vibepro-one-command-pr-ready-closure acceptance coverage`, whose assertions match only hard-coded Japanese prose for AC1-7 and S-001/S-003/S-005. It does not read Story clauses or exercise production behavior, yet .vibepro/qa/e2e-e7ce2b31.json reports 5/5 and the E2E verification record calls those five tests public CLI scenarios covering S-001 through S-005 and AC-1 through AC-8. The four substantive E2E tests plus focused suites provide real evidence for the contract, but this fifth test makes the persisted count and coverage description circular and overstated. Remove the hard-coded acceptance test (or replace every marker with a behavioral/repository assertion), update the artifact/ledger to the truthful substantive test count and exact scenario set, then rerun E2E and current-head Gate/adjudication.
